input.background: Table 3.9 lists the percent of residents in the 50 states who identified themselves in the 2000 census as Spanish, Hispanic, or Latino.
input.data:
\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 3.9 Data for Exercise 3.11. Percent of residents who identified themselves as Spanish, Hispanic, or Latino by state, U.S., 2000.}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline State & Percent & State & Percent & State & Percent \
\hline Alabama & 1.5 & Louisiana & 2.4 & Ohio & 1.9 \
\hline Alaska & 4.1 & Maine & . 7 & Oklahoma & 5.2 \
\hline Arizona & 25.3 & Maryland & 4.3 & Oregon & 8.0 \
\hline Arkansas & 2.8 & Massachusetts & 6.8 & Pennsylvania & 3.2 \
\hline California & 32.4 & Michigan & 3.3 & Rhode Island & 8.7 \
\hline Colorado & 17.1 & Minnesota & 2.9 & South Carolina & 2.4 \
\hline Connecticut & 9.4 & Mississippi & 1.3 & South Dakota & 1.4 \
\hline Delaware & 4.8 & Missouri & 2.1 & Tennessee & 2.0 \
\hline Florida & 16.8 & Montana & 2.0 & Texas & 32.0 \
\hline Georgia & 5.3 & Nebraska & 5.5 & Utah & 9.0 \
\hline Hawaii & 7.2 & Nevada & 19.7 & Vermont & 0.9 \
\hline Idaho & 7.9 & New Hampshire & 1.7 & Virginia & 4.7 \
\hline Illinois & 10.7 & New Jersey & 13.3 & Washington & 7.2 \
\hline Indiana & 3.5 & New Mexico & 42.1 & West Virginia & 0.7 \
\hline Iowa & 2.8 & New York & 15.1 & Wisconsin & 3.6 \
\hline Kansas & 7.0 & North Carolina & 4.7 & Wyoming & 6.4 \
\hline Kentucky & 1.5 & North Dakota & 1.2 & & \
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
input.question: Create a stemplot of these data using single stem values and an axis multiplier of $\times 10$.

State.description: The name of each U.S. state.

Percent.description: The percentage of residents in each state who identified themselves as Spanish, Hispanic, or Latino in the 2000 census.
